Jekyll Portfolio Website

This repo contains a working static website written with Jekyll, integrated with content coming from a DatoCMS administrative area.

Usage

First, install the dependencies of this project:

yarn install

Then, to run this website in development mode (with live-reload):

yarn start

To build the final, production ready static website:

yarn build

The final result will be saved in the public directory.

About

The goal of this project is to show how easily you can create static sites using the content (text, images, links, etc.) stored on DatoCMS. This project is configured to fetch data from a specific administrative area using the API DatoCMS provides.

This websites uses:

  • Yarn as package manager;
  • Webpack to compile and bundle assets (Sass/ES2015 JS);
  • dato gem to integrate the website with DatoCMS.

The dato.config.rb file

To convert the content stored on DatoCMS into local Markdown files that can be digested by Jekyll, the datocms-client plugin requires an explicit mapping file called dato.config.js. You can read more about the commands available in this file in the plugin documentation.
